steps.title: Spot the requirement for the proxy to work
steps.content: For L. stagnalis to serve as a reliable proxy for X. laevis bioaccumulation, the two species must show comparable bioaccumulation patterns and rates.

steps.title: Find what breaks that requirement
steps.content:
Choice B directly weakens the hypothesis: if uptake rates differ between species in ways researchers don't yet understand, then L. stagnalis bioaccumulation cannot reliably represent X. laevis bioaccumulation.

Choices A and D support the hypothesis (consistent patterns and practical benefits), while C shifts to broader nanoparticle profiles rather than proxy validity.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
